# Gas Saturation

**Summary**: The fraction of pore volume occupied by gas.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2019-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2019]] Not applicable.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] used gas-saturation to determine the pay zone and evaluate inversion accuracy.

- [[2021-suleymanov|Suleymanov, 2021]] used gas-saturation to evaluate the impact of fluid changes on seismic attributes.

## Scientific Context

- [[2019-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2019]] Petrophysical property.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] used gas-saturation to determine the pay zone and evaluate inversion accuracy.

- [[2021-suleymanov|Suleymanov, 2021]] used gas-saturation to evaluate the impact of fluid changes on seismic attributes.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Not stated in source ([[2019-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2019]])


- Gas saturation derived from neural network guided inversion was more reliable than rock physics modeling. ([[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019, p. 101]])

- Fluid substitution was used to remove the effects of gas and oil to isolate mineralogical impacts. ([[2021-suleymanov|Suleymanov, 2021, p. 50]])
